artifacts.title: Three-Way Selected-Skeleton Coherence
artifacts.summary: CORRECTED 2026-07-16: global three-arm natural isomorphism at C_2 REFUTED by the wall-isotropy/stabilizer obstruction; forks into C2a chamber coherence (open) and C2b wall-isotropy comparison theorem (proved). Lorentzian gauge-selection branch refuted by divisibility of ker(Sigma). Remaining: two typed functors on the regular chamber + precision two-cover resolution.

claims.statement: The two DBP-arm reduction functors (founding role-channel arm; precision-flow PRED-006 arm) admit construction into the same typed selected skeleton as the AC-fold R3 arm, on the regular chamber.

REFUTED AS STATED: the three selected-skeleton images are NOT globally naturally isomorphic at C_2. Stabilizer obstruction: founding/AC wall points have Stab=C_2, precision output-tie points have Stab={1}; equivariant isomorphisms preserve isotropy, so no wall-preserving C_2-equivariant isomorphism exists. Forks into C2a and C2b.

Chamber coherence: the three arms' regular-chamber restrictions are equivalent as C_2-torsor bundles with sections (walls excluded, no isotropy obstruction). Requires the two typed functors (C1) plus the precision-flow two-cover resolution (state cover vs output bundle).

Wall-isotropy comparison theorem: founding and AC-fold walls are fixed-point type (Stab=C_2, canonical real section extending to the double root); the precision-flow free-orbit obstruction lives in the output bundle only (state/account cover is fixed-point type); one convention bit is necessary and sufficient at a free two-point fibre (round-half-even supplies it via significand parity); refinement is strictly selection-preserving off double-rounding coarse-tie preimages and strictly fails on them; lax naturality is undefined pending a defect-enriched 2-category.

REFUTED: the Lorentzian gauge-selection branch. The effective gauge group V=ker(Sigma) is divisible (every a=2b), so every homomorphism V->C_2 is trivial; the gauge action cannot produce a two-sheeted quotient. The Lorentzian (2,1) form governs channel allocation (null vs transverse directions), orthogonal to sheet selection.

relations.basis: The wall-isotropy theorem is the proved fork C2b of the campaign's headline claim; source doc WALL_ISOTROPY_COMPARISON_THEOREM_2026-07-16.md, sha256 8d080e07fc1feda079fcd0bcd9f01adfb1596368e690d4874b89163b24fdfae7.
AC-fold R3 supplies the typed selected skeleton; its wall is fixed-point type per the isotropy theorem.
Kernel-2Z non-faithfulness is the common C_2 floor for the chamber-level comparison; walls excluded by the isotropy obstruction.
P4's free-orbit obstruction is located in the precision output bundle; the wall-isotropy classification depends on PRED-006's two-cover split.

declared_impacts.note: C2 refuted as stated and forked into C2a (open) and C2b (proved). Lorentzian branch banked as refuted claim C3. U-1918 summary corrected. Campaign assessment changes from nearly-closed to corrected fork.

llm.one_sentence_purpose: Classify the three CAND-001 realizations by wall isotropy: global C_2 isomorphism refuted by stabilizer mismatch; chamber coherence open; wall-isotropy comparison theorem proved.
retrieval_questions: Why is the global three-arm isomorphism impossible?
Which arms have fixed-point vs free-orbit walls?
Why does IEEE-754 need a tie convention when GR does not?

extensions.assessed: global-iso refuted; wall-isotropy classification proved; chamber form open pending functors; lax form pending defect-enriched category
extensions.remaining_work: C1 typed functors on the regular chamber + precision two-cover resolution; optional: define the defect-enriched 2-category for the lax statement

upsert_nodes.label: DBP-arm reduction functors and three-way selected-skeleton coherence
upsert_nodes.summary: Construct the two DBP-arm functors into the same typed selected skeleton used by the AC fold, then prove componentwise well-definedness, fullness, faithfulness, essential surjectivity, and the comparison diagrams. The AC-fold construction alone does not establish a three-way natural isomorphism.
